Implement EXACTLY ONE task end-to-end in your isolated git worktree, run the gate, commit on the task branch, return the structured result JSON. Do NOT mutate the ledger; do NOT merge.

Task id: T78 (ledgerRefs goals:G5)
Branch: implement/T78    Base commit: 356d850 (current main HEAD). If cut from a stale HEAD, `git reset --hard 356d850` first; confirm test/realhosts/ exists and internal/config has the [dns]/per-peer dns opt-in (G5) before starting.
headline: Realhosts report-only dial-by-name check (stretch)
description: Q36: in scope only as a report-only stretch per the M10/Q12 discipline. Extend test/realhosts (runner.go harness): bring the standing two-host testbed up with the edge dialing the concentrator by its real DNS name (o3.7mind.io resolves to the standing ConcPubIP) with the dns opt-in enabled, and REPORT (never fail the suite on) the outcome: resolved address, time-to-first-handshake, and steady traffic. No mid-session IP change on real hosts (the public IP is fixed); this tier only proves the resolve-then-dial path against real resolvers and real NAT. Keep it strictly report-only: any failure logs a report line, exit status stays green, matching the existing realhosts discipline.
acceptance: go test -tags realhosts ./test/realhosts/... runs against the standing testbed and emits the dial-by-name report (resolution result + handshake + traffic outcome); an induced failure (bogus name) demonstrably does NOT fail the suite — it reports and passes.

IMPORTANT: the `-tags realhosts` suite runs against the STANDING two-host testbed (o3.7mind.io + the concentrator) and is not runnable from this sandbox — write the report-only check so it COMPILES and vets under `-tags realhosts` (`go vet -tags realhosts ./test/realhosts/...` + `go build -tags realhosts ./test/realhosts/...` must pass); note execution is deferred to the realhosts run. Read test/realhosts/runner.go for the EXISTING report-only discipline (how it logs report lines and keeps exit status green) and MATCH it exactly — the key acceptance property is that a failure REPORTS but does NOT fail the suite.

Gate (run via `nix develop -c sh -c '<cmd>'`): `go build ./... && go vet ./... && go vet -tags realhosts ./test/realhosts/... && go build -tags realhosts ./test/realhosts/... && go test ./...`. gofmt your changes.
If your worktree is not reachable for edits, `git switch -c t78-fix` off 356d850 in your own worktree via the shared .git store, commit, report the EXACT sha. Return {taskId, status, resultCommit, branch, filesTouched, checkSummary, summary, blockedReason?} with the exact resultCommit sha.

- baboon: Baboon data model compiler reference. Covers model syntax, multi-version evolution, code generation, wire-format conversions, database migrations, backward compatibility, and version negotiation. TRIGGER when: designing, changing, or discussing any external API, working with .baboon model files, updating data models, adding new fields or types, bumping model versions, running code generation, implementing conversions, writing database migrations, or discussing API versioning strategy.
- constructive-test-taxonomy: Multi-axis classification of tests (Intention × Encapsulation × Isolation) for evaluating test quality and steering test-writing decisions. Replaces fuzzy Unit/Integration/E2E vocabulary with precise tags and a maintenance-cost heuristic. TRIGGER when: writing new tests, reviewing a test suite, deciding what to test or at what level, discussing mocks vs. real dependencies, classifying tests, or arguing about test coverage or test-strategy tradeoffs.
- dual-tests: Pattern for testing code that touches external systems: write one abstract test suite against an interface, then run it against both the production adapter (real DB/API/FS) and a hand-written in-memory dummy. Prefers manual dummies over auto-generated mocks. TRIGGER when: designing code that depends on a database, HTTP API, message queue, filesystem, or other external system; choosing between mocks, fakes, or dummies; adding integration points; setting up test infrastructure; or when the `constructive-test-taxonomy` skill calls for moving Communication-tier tests toward Blackbox-Atomic.
- environment: Bubblewrap "yolo" sandbox escape workflow (exchange scripts for reaching $HOME, system paths, or files outside the sandbox) plus in-sandbox nix/direnv usage. TRIGGER only when $SMIND_SANDBOXED=1 — i.e. running inside the yolo sandbox and you need to reach something outside it. SKIP entirely when $SMIND_SANDBOXED is unset: you are unsandboxed, so write directly and ignore this skill.
- izumi: Best-practice playbook for the izumi Scala ecosystem — distage (DI), distage-framework (roles/RoleAppMain), distage-testkit (test harness with memoization), distage-framework-docker (test containers), logstage (structured logging), and BIO (bifunctor effect typeclasses). Covers idiomatic bindings, Activation/Axis switching, Lifecycle discipline, plugin layout, and how to apply the `dual-tests` pattern inside distage so one test body runs against both a dummy and a real-DB adapter. TRIGGER when: writing or reviewing Scala code that imports `izumi.distage.*`, `izumi.logstage.*`, `izumi.functional.bio.*`, `distage.*`, `logstage.*`; designing a new distage plugin or Role; wiring a testkit `SpecZIO`/`Spec2`/`Spec1`; configuring `ContainerDef`/`DockerSupportModule`; choosing between `StandardAxis.Repo` or `Scene` tags; or implementing the dual-tests pattern in a distage project. The canonical reference project is https://github.com/7mind/distage-example.

Invoke ONLY when the user explicitly references this skill by name (e.g. "/resilient-ws-ui", "use the resilient-ws-ui skill"). Do NOT auto-invoke based on topic match.
- tass: Target/Actual State Separation (TASS) pattern for reliable state management in event-driven control systems. - cq:advance: Advance the WHOLE flow one cycle — chain /cq:investigate:advance, /cq:plan:advance, /cq:implement:advance to quiescence, then report DRAINED / BLOCKED-ON-QUESTIONS / MIXED.
- cq:implement-review: Implement-flow adversarial per-task review rubric + stdout-JSON contract, as a portable promptTemplate for non-Claude harnesses (pi/Codex). Symmetric to cq:plan-review. Judges ONE task's implementation, splits findings into autonomously-fixable criticism[], user-only questions[], and out-of-scope defects[], and emits the verdict as JSON on stdout. Writes NOTHING to the ledger — the orchestrator records the terminal review.
- cq:implement:advance: Advance an implement-flow run one full pass — pick DAG-ready tasks, implement each in an isolated worktree, adversarially review, autonomously fix criticism, register user questions, and merge back in dependency order.
- cq:implement:start: Start an implement-flow run — resolve the target milestones (default: all open ones), validate their task DAG, then hand off to the /cq:implement:advance loop.
- cq:investigate: Start an investigate-flow run — defect intake + bootstrap, then run the /cq:investigate:advance pass inline.
- cq:investigate:advance: Advance an investigate-flow run one research round — read defect state, form/extend the hypothesis tree, dispatch read-only explorers, validate every citation against source, adjudicate node status, and on a confirmed root cause file-and-defer the fix to plan-flow.

The CANONICAL rubric source; Claude's plan-reviewer agent and every non-Claude (Codex/Pi) reviewer judge the same way and emit the same JSON.
- cq:plan:advance: Advance plan-flow goals one full round — a given goal, or (no argument) every unlocked goal — running the planner↔reviewer loop until each needs the user or reaches `planned`.
- cq:plan:follow-up: Add new scope to an EXISTING plan-flow goal — append the follow-up request, re-open the goal, and hand to the planner for a fresh clarifying round.

- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. - update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.

1	//go:build realhosts
2	
3	package realhosts
4	
5	import (
6		"bytes"
7		"context"
8		"fmt"
9		"os"
10		"os/exec"
11		"strconv"
12		"strings"
13		"time"
14	)
15	
16	// Environment variables that select the real hosts and credentials. Each has a
17	// default (below) so the tier runs against the standing worker machines with no
18	// configuration.
19	const (
20		envEdgeHost  = "WANBOND_EDGE_HOST"
21		envConcHost  = "WANBOND_CONC_HOST"
22		envConcPubIP = "WANBOND_CONC_PUBLIP"
23		envSSHKey    = "WANBOND_SSH_KEY"
24	)
25	
26	// Defaults for the standing two-machine testbed:
27	//   - the edge sits behind a symmetric NAT and is amd64;
28	//   - the concentrator is aarch64 and has a public, inbound-reachable IP.
29	const (
30		defaultEdgeHost  = "llm-ubuntu-0.pgtr.7mind.io"
31		defaultConcHost  = "o3.7mind.io"
32		defaultConcPubIP = "89.168.124.91"
33		defaultSSHKey    = "/run/agenix/llm-ssh-key"
34	
35		// defaultUser is the login shared by both worker machines.
36		defaultUser = "ubuntu"
37	
38		// defaultConnectTimeout bounds the TCP+auth handshake of a single ssh
39		// invocation. The runner passes it as `-o ConnectTimeout=<n>` (seconds).
40		defaultConnectTimeout = 10 * time.Second
41	)
42	
43	// Host is one real machine reachable over SSH.
44	type Host struct {
45		// Role is a human label for the machine's function ("edge"/"concentrator").
46		Role string
47		// Addr is the SSH target hostname (or IP) passed to ssh.
48		Addr string
49		// User is the SSH login user.
50		User string
51	}
52	
53	// target renders the user@host argument ssh expects.
54	func (h Host) target() string { return h.User + "@" + h.Addr }
55	
56	// Config is the resolved real-host topology plus SSH credentials, populated from
57	// the environment (with defaults) by LoadConfig.
58	type Config struct {
59		Edge Host
60		Conc Host
61		// ConcPubIP is the concentrator's public, inbound-reachable IP. The edge
62		// (behind symmetric NAT) dials this; recorded here for later tasks.
63		ConcPubIP string
64		// SSHKey is the private-key path handed to `ssh -i`.
65		SSHKey string
66		// ConnectTimeout bounds each ssh connect.
67		ConnectTimeout time.Duration
68	}
69	
70	// envOr returns the value of env var key, or def when it is unset or empty.
71	func envOr(key, def string) string {
72		if v := os.Getenv(key); v != "" {
73			return v
74		}
75		return def
76	}
77	
78	// LoadConfig resolves the real-host topology from the environment, applying the
79	// standing-testbed defaults for any unset variable. Both hosts use the default
80	// SSH login user.
81	func LoadConfig() Config {
82		return Config{
83			Edge:           Host{Role: "edge", Addr: envOr(envEdgeHost, defaultEdgeHost), User: defaultUser},
84			Conc:           Host{Role: "concentrator", Addr: envOr(envConcHost, defaultConcHost), User: defaultUser},
85			ConcPubIP:      envOr(envConcPubIP, defaultConcPubIP),
86			SSHKey:         envOr(envSSHKey, defaultSSHKey),
87			ConnectTimeout: defaultConnectTimeout,
88		}
89	}
90	
91	// Result captures the outcome of one remote command.
92	type Result struct {
93		Host   Host
94		Cmd    string
95		Stdout string
96		Stderr string
97	}
98	
99	// Runner executes commands on real hosts over SSH. It is safe for sequential
100	// use; the SSH invocation itself carries no shared state.
101	type Runner struct {
102		key            string
103		connectTimeout time.Duration
104	}
105	
106	// NewRunner builds a Runner from a Config's key and connect timeout.
107	func NewRunner(cfg Config) *Runner {
108		return &Runner{key: cfg.SSHKey, connectTimeout: cfg.ConnectTimeout}
109	}
110	
111	// sshArgs assembles the argv for a single ssh invocation against host running
112	// remoteCmd. The system ssh_config is known-broken on these workers, so `-F
113	// none` forces ssh to ignore it; the remaining options pin the identity file,
114	// auto-accept the host key on first contact, and bound the connect.
115	func (r *Runner) sshArgs(host Host, remoteCmd string) []string {
116		secs := int(r.connectTimeout / time.Second)
117		if secs < 1 {
118			secs = 1
119		}
120		return []string{
121			"-F", "none",
122			"-i", r.key,
123			"-o", "StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new",
124			"-o", "ConnectTimeout=" + strconv.Itoa(secs),
125			host.target(),
126			remoteCmd,
127		}
128	}
129	
130	// Run executes remoteCmd on host over SSH, capturing stdout and stderr
131	// separately. A non-nil error wraps the exit status and includes the captured
132	// stderr for diagnosis; ctx bounds the whole invocation.
133	func (r *Runner) Run(ctx context.Context, host Host, remoteCmd string) (Result, error) {
134		cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "ssh", r.sshArgs(host, remoteCmd)...)
135		var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer
136		cmd.Stdout, cmd.Stderr = &stdout, &stderr
137	
138		res := Result{Host: host, Cmd: remoteCmd}
139		err := cmd.Run()
140		res.Stdout, res.Stderr = strings.TrimRight(stdout.String(), "\n"), strings.TrimRight(stderr.String(), "\n")
141		if err != nil {
142			return res, fmt.Errorf("ssh %s: %q: %w\nstderr: %s", host.target(), remoteCmd, err, res.Stderr)
143		}
144		return res, nil
145	}

1	//go:build realhosts
2	
3	package realhosts
4	
5	import (
6		"context"
7		"testing"
8		"time"
9	)
10	
11	// connectivityTimeout is the shared budget for one host's connectivity check
12	// (both `uname` round-trips run under the same context). Generous relative to
13	// ConnectTimeout to tolerate a cold SSH session.
14	const connectivityTimeout = 30 * time.Second
15	
16	// TestRealConnectivity is the report-only entry point of the real-host tier: it
17	// runs a trivial command (`uname -a`) on BOTH the edge and the concentrator via
18	// the SSH runner and records each host's kernel/arch line. It asserts only that
19	// the command succeeds — executing and recording IS the acceptance (per Q12);
20	// it gates nothing.
21	func TestRealConnectivity(t *testing.T) {
22		cfg := LoadConfig()
23		runner := NewRunner(cfg)
24	
25		t.Logf("realhosts config: edge=%s concentrator=%s conc-public-ip=%s ssh-key=%s",
26			cfg.Edge.target(), cfg.Conc.target(), cfg.ConcPubIP, cfg.SSHKey)
27	
28		for _, host := range []Host{cfg.Edge, cfg.Conc} {
29			host := host
30			t.Run(host.Role, func(t *testing.T) {
31				ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), connectivityTimeout)
32				defer cancel()
33	
34				res, err := runner.Run(ctx, host, "uname -a")
35				if err != nil {
36					t.Fatalf("%s (%s): connectivity check failed: %v", host.Role, host.target(), err)
37				}
38				t.Logf("%s (%s) uname: %s", host.Role, host.target(), res.Stdout)
39	
40				arch, err := runner.Run(ctx, host, "uname -m")
41				if err != nil {
42					t.Fatalf("%s (%s): arch probe failed: %v", host.Role, host.target(), err)
43				}
44				t.Logf("%s (%s) arch: %s", host.Role, host.target(), arch.Stdout)
45			})
46		}
47	}

1	//go:build realhosts
2	
3	package realhosts
4	
5	import (
6		"context"
7		"crypto/ecdh"
8		"crypto/rand"
9		"encoding/base64"
10		"encoding/json"
11		"fmt"
12		"io"
13		"os"
14		"os/exec"
15		"path/filepath"
16		"regexp"
17		"strconv"
18		"strings"
19		"testing"
20		"time"
21	)
22	
23	// P0 smoke overlay addressing and wire parameters. The inner /24 mirrors the
24	// netns e2e fixture (test/e2e/p0_test.go): edge=10.10.0.1, concentrator=10.10.0.2.
25	const (
26		smokeEdgeInner  = "10.10.0.1"
27		smokeConcInner  = "10.10.0.2"
28		smokeInnerCIDR  = "24"
29		smokeListenPort = 51820
30	
31		// smokeRemoteDir is where the repo is synced and the native wanbond binary is
32		// built on each host. Both hosts share the login (defaultUser) home layout.
33		smokeRemoteDir = "/home/" + defaultUser + "/wanbond-smoke"
34		smokeBin       = smokeRemoteDir + "/wanbond"
35	
36		// smokeUnit is the transient systemd unit the wanbond daemon runs under on
37		// each host; iperfUnit is the concentrator's iperf3 server unit. Distinct
38		// machines, so the daemon unit name may repeat across hosts.
39		smokeUnit = "wanbond-smoke"
40		iperfUnit = "wanbond-iperf3"
41	)
42	
43	// Timeouts. The tier is report-only but drives real internet round-trips and a
44	// native Go build, so budgets are generous.
45	const (
46		smokeSSHTimeout   = 60 * time.Second
47		smokeBuildTimeout = 5 * time.Minute
48		smokeSyncTimeout  = 3 * time.Minute
49		linkAppearTimeout = 20 * time.Second
50		handshakeTimeout  = 45 * time.Second
51		iperfRunTimeout   = 60 * time.Second
52		cleanupTimeout    = 30 * time.Second
53	)
54	
55	// TestRealP0Smoke codifies the manually validated single-uplink P0 flow against
56	// the two standing worker machines: provision both hosts, natively build
57	// wanbond on each, bring a plain-WireGuard tunnel up over the real internet
58	// path (edge behind symmetric NAT -> concentrator public IP), and record the
59	// handshake, ping RTT, and three iperf3 measurements (single TCP, 8x-parallel
60	// TCP, UDP goodput/loss). REPORT-ONLY per Q12: executing and recording IS the
61	// acceptance; no threshold gates it. Every remote resource it starts is torn
62	// down on exit, including on failure, via t.Cleanup.
63	func TestRealP0Smoke(t *testing.T) {
64		cfg := LoadConfig()
65		r := NewRunner(cfg)
66	
67		t.Logf("realhosts config: edge=%s concentrator=%s conc-public-ip=%s ssh-key=%s",
68			cfg.Edge.target(), cfg.Conc.target(), cfg.ConcPubIP, cfg.SSHKey)
69	
70		// 1. Provision both hosts (idempotent: a no-op on an already-provisioned host).
71		//    The concentrator additionally gets the tunnel-interface INPUT ACCEPT rule.
72		provision(t, r, cfg.Edge, ProvisionOpts{})
73		provision(t, r, cfg.Conc, ProvisionOpts{TunnelIface: tunnelIface})
74	
75		// 2. Sync the repo and build wanbond natively on each host (arch matches host).
76		//    Register removal of smokeRemoteDir immediately after each sync so the
77		//    synced repo, the native binary, and the secret configs written into it in
78		//    step 5 are torn down on exit — including on failure/panic. Key material
79		//    must not outlive the tunnel it secures.
80		root := repoRoot(t)
81		syncAndBuild(t, r, cfg.Edge, root)
82		t.Cleanup(func() { removeRemoteDir(t, r, cfg.Edge) })
83		syncAndBuild(t, r, cfg.Conc, root)
84		t.Cleanup(func() { removeRemoteDir(t, r, cfg.Conc) })
85	
86		// 3. Key material: an X25519 keypair per end plus a shared PSK (base64), same
87		//    scheme as the netns e2e fixture.
88		edgePriv, edgePub := genSmokeKey(t)
89		concPriv, concPub := genSmokeKey(t)
90		psk := randSmokeKey(t)
91	
92		// 4. Each path binds its socket to the host's primary source IP.
93		edgeSrc := primaryIP(t, r, cfg.Edge)
94		concSrc := primaryIP(t, r, cfg.Conc)
95		t.Logf("path source addrs: edge=%s concentrator=%s", edgeSrc, concSrc)
96	
97		// 5. Write the 0600 configs (Amnezia omitted -> plain WireGuard). The
98		//    concentrator listens; the edge dials the concentrator's PUBLIC IP.
99		concCfg := fmt.Sprintf(`role = "concentrator"
100	psk = "%s"
101	
102	[[paths]]
103	name = "wan0"
104	source_addr = "%s"
105	
106	[wireguard]
107	private_key = "%s"
108	listen_port = %d
109	
110	[[wireguard.peers]]
111	public_key = "%s"
112	allowed_ips = ["%s/32"]
113	
114	[log]
115	level = "info"
116	`, psk, concSrc, concPriv, smokeListenPort, edgePub, smokeEdgeInner)
117	
118		edgeCfg := fmt.Sprintf(`role = "edge"
119	psk = "%s"
120	
121	[[paths]]
122	name = "wan0"
123	source_addr = "%s"
124	
125	[wireguard]
126	private_key = "%s"
127	
128	[[wireguard.peers]]
129	public_key = "%s"
130	endpoint = "%s:%d"
131	allowed_ips = ["%s/32"]
132	
133	[log]
134	level = "info"
135	`, psk, edgeSrc, edgePriv, concPub, cfg.ConcPubIP, smokeListenPort, smokeConcInner)
136	
137		concCfgPath := smokeRemoteDir + "/conc.toml"
138		edgeCfgPath := smokeRemoteDir + "/edge.toml"
139		writeRemoteFile(t, r, cfg.Conc, concCfgPath, concCfg)
140		writeRemoteFile(t, r, cfg.Edge, edgeCfgPath, edgeCfg)
141	
142		// 6. Clear any leftover unit/interface from a prior interrupted run, then
143		//    start the concentrator first (it must be listening before the edge
144		//    initiates), then the edge, each as a transient systemd unit for clean
145		//    detachment. Register teardown immediately so a later failure still stops
146		//    the daemon and removes the interface.
147		preClean(t, r, cfg.Conc)
148		preClean(t, r, cfg.Edge)
149		startDaemon(t, r, cfg.Conc, concCfgPath)
150		startDaemon(t, r, cfg.Edge, edgeCfgPath)
151	
152		// 7. Wait for each TUN to appear, then address it (wanbond owns the engine
153		//    only; addressing is the operator's job).
154		if !waitRemoteLink(t, r, cfg.Conc, tunnelIface, linkAppearTimeout) {
155			dumpDaemonLog(t, r, cfg.Conc)
156			t.Fatalf("concentrator %s never appeared", tunnelIface)
157		}
158		if !waitRemoteLink(t, r, cfg.Edge, tunnelIface, linkAppearTimeout) {
159			dumpDaemonLog(t, r, cfg.Edge)
160			t.Fatalf("edge %s never appeared", tunnelIface)
161		}
162		addressLink(t, r, cfg.Conc, smokeConcInner)
163		addressLink(t, r, cfg.Edge, smokeEdgeInner)
164	
165		// 8. From the edge, ping the concentrator's inner IP. The first packet drives
166		//    the WireGuard handshake through the NAT; the first reply means the
167		//    session is established.
168		if !pingUntil(t, r, cfg.Edge, smokeConcInner, handshakeTimeout) {
169			dumpDaemonLog(t, r, cfg.Conc)
170			dumpDaemonLog(t, r, cfg.Edge)
171			t.Fatalf("handshake never completed: %s unreachable from the edge through the tunnel", smokeConcInner)
172		}
173		rttMs := measureRTT(t, r, cfg.Edge, smokeConcInner)
174		t.Logf("HANDSHAKE OK; ping %s from edge: avg RTT = %.3f ms", smokeConcInner, rttMs)
175	
176		// 9. iperf3 server on the concentrator (bound to its inner IP), then three
177		//    client runs from the edge.
178		startIperfServer(t, r, cfg.Conc, smokeConcInner)
179	
180		single := iperfTCP(t, r, cfg.Edge, smokeConcInner, 1)
181		t.Logf("iperf3 single-flow TCP: %.2f Mbit/s (retransmits=%d)", single.mbps, single.retransmits)
182	
183		parallel := iperfTCP(t, r, cfg.Edge, smokeConcInner, 8)
184		t.Logf("iperf3 8x-parallel TCP: %.2f Mbit/s (retransmits=%d)", parallel.mbps, parallel.retransmits)
185	
186		udp := iperfUDP(t, r, cfg.Edge, smokeConcInner, "100M")
187		t.Logf("iperf3 UDP @100M: goodput %.2f Mbit/s, loss %.3f%%, jitter %.3f ms", udp.mbps, udp.lossPct, udp.jitterMs)
188	
189		// Final report block (report-only; nothing below gates the test).
190		t.Logf("=== P0 SMOKE RESULTS ===\n"+
191			"  handshake:      OK\n"+
192			"  ping avg RTT:   %.3f ms\n"+
193			"  TCP single:     %.2f Mbit/s (retx %d)\n"+
194			"  TCP 8-parallel: %.2f Mbit/s (retx %d)\n"+
195			"  UDP goodput:    %.2f Mbit/s (loss %.3f%%, jitter %.3f ms)",
196			rttMs, single.mbps, single.retransmits, parallel.mbps, parallel.retransmits,
197			udp.mbps, udp.lossPct, udp.jitterMs)
198	}
199	
200	// provision runs the T32 idempotent provisioner against host, failing the test
201	// on error and logging the per-step outcome.
202	func provision(t *testing.T, r *Runner, host Host, opts ProvisionOpts) {
203		t.Helper()
204		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), provisionTimeout)
205		defer cancel()
206		rep, err := Provision(ctx, r, host, opts)
207		if err != nil {
208			t.Fatalf("%s: provisioning failed: %v", host.Role, err)
209		}
210		t.Logf("provisioned %s", rep)
211	}
212	
213	// repoRoot returns the module root by walking up from the test's working
214	// directory (the package dir) until it finds go.mod.
215	func repoRoot(t *testing.T) string {
216		t.Helper()
217		dir, err := os.Getwd()
218		if err != nil {
219			t.Fatalf("getwd: %v", err)
220		}
221		for {
222			if _, err := os.Stat(filepath.Join(dir, "go.mod")); err == nil {
223				return dir
224			}
225			parent := filepath.Dir(dir)
226			if parent == dir {
227				t.Fatalf("go.mod not found walking up from working directory")
228			}
229			dir = parent
230		}
231	}
232	
233	// syncAndBuild tar-streams the repo (excluding VCS/ledger dirs) into
234	// smokeRemoteDir on host, then builds wanbond there with the pinned Go
235	// toolchain. The build arch matches the host's arch (native build).
236	func syncAndBuild(t *testing.T, r *Runner, host Host, root string) {
237		t.Helper()
238	
239		// Fresh directory, then extract the streamed tar.
240		syncCtx, cancelSync := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), smokeSyncTimeout)
241		defer cancelSync()
242		remoteCmd := "rm -rf " + smokeRemoteDir + " && mkdir -p " + smokeRemoteDir + " && tar xzf - -C " + smokeRemoteDir
243		ssh := exec.CommandContext(syncCtx, "ssh", r.sshArgs(host, remoteCmd)...)
244		tar := exec.CommandContext(syncCtx, "tar", "czf", "-",
245			"--exclude=./.git", "--exclude=./.cq", "--exclude=./.direnv",
246			"--exclude=./.github", "--exclude=./wanbond",
247			"--exclude=./.claude", "--exclude=./.codegraph",
248			"--exclude=./.worktrees", "--exclude=./result", "-C", root, ".")
249	
250		tarOut, err := tar.StdoutPipe()
251		if err != nil {
252			t.Fatalf("%s: tar stdout pipe: %v", host.Role, err)
253		}
254		ssh.Stdin = tarOut
255		var tarErr, sshErr strings.Builder
256		tar.Stderr = &tarErr
257		ssh.Stderr = &sshErr
258	
259		if err := ssh.Start(); err != nil {
260			t.Fatalf("%s: start ssh (sync): %v", host.Role, err)
261		}
262		if err := tar.Run(); err != nil {
263			t.Fatalf("%s: tar (sync): %v\n%s", host.Role, err, tarErr.String())
264		}
265		if err := ssh.Wait(); err != nil {
266			t.Fatalf("%s: ssh extract (sync): %v\n%s", host.Role, err, sshErr.String())
267		}
268		t.Logf("%s: repo synced to %s", host.Role, smokeRemoteDir)
269	
270		// Native build with the provisioned Go toolchain (gcc kept on PATH for cgo).
271		buildCtx, cancelBuild := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), smokeBuildTimeout)
272		defer cancelBuild()
273		build := "cd " + smokeRemoteDir + " && PATH=" + goInstallDir + "/bin:$PATH go build -o wanbond ./cmd/wanbond"
274		if _, err := r.Run(buildCtx, host, build); err != nil {
275			t.Fatalf("%s: native wanbond build failed: %v", host.Role, err)
276		}
277		t.Logf("%s: built %s", host.Role, smokeBin)
278	}
279	
280	// primaryIP returns host's primary IPv4 source address (the src of its default
281	// route), which each path's socket binds to.
282	func primaryIP(t *testing.T, r *Runner, host Host) string {
283		t.Helper()
284		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), smokeSSHTimeout)
285		defer cancel()
286		res, err := r.Run(ctx, host, `ip route get 1.1.1.1 | sed -n 's/.*src \([0-9.]*\).*/\1/p' | head -1`)
287		if err != nil {
288			t.Fatalf("%s: primary IP probe failed: %v", host.Role, err)
289		}
290		ip := strings.TrimSpace(res.Stdout)
291		if ip == "" {
292			t.Fatalf("%s: could not determine primary IP", host.Role)
293		}
294		return ip
295	}
296	
297	// writeRemoteFile pipes content to path on host at mode 0600 (the exact mode
298	// config.Load requires), failing the test on error. `umask 077` narrows the
299	// creation mode BEFORE any secret bytes are written, so the file is never
300	// briefly world-readable; the trailing chmod is belt-and-braces.
301	func writeRemoteFile(t *testing.T, r *Runner, host Host, path, content string) {
302		t.Helper()
303		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), smokeSSHTimeout)
304		defer cancel()
305		remoteCmd := "umask 077 && cat > " + path + " && chmod 600 " + path
306		cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "ssh", r.sshArgs(host, remoteCmd)...)
307		cmd.Stdin = strings.NewReader(content)
308		var stderr strings.Builder
309		cmd.Stderr = &stderr
310		if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
311			t.Fatalf("%s: write %s failed: %v\n%s", host.Role, path, err, stderr.String())
312		}
313		t.Logf("%s: wrote %s (0600)", host.Role, path)
314	}
315	
316	// startDaemon launches the wanbond daemon on host under a transient systemd unit
317	// (clean detachment; nohup-& would hang the SSH session) and registers teardown
318	// (stop the unit, remove the interface) that runs even on failure.
319	func startDaemon(t *testing.T, r *Runner, host Host, cfgPath string) {
320		t.Helper()
321		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), smokeSSHTimeout)
322		defer cancel()
323	
324		// Register teardown BEFORE start so a failed start still cleans up.
325		t.Cleanup(func() {
326			stopUnit(t, r, host, smokeUnit)
327			delLink(t, r, host, tunnelIface)
328		})
329	
330		start := fmt.Sprintf("sudo systemd-run --unit=%s --service-type=simple %s --config %s",
331			smokeUnit, smokeBin, cfgPath)
332		if _, err := r.Run(ctx, host, start); err != nil {
333			t.Fatalf("%s: start wanbond daemon failed: %v", host.Role, err)
334		}
335		t.Logf("%s: wanbond daemon started (unit=%s)", host.Role, smokeUnit)
336	}
337	
338	// waitRemoteLink polls until dev exists on host or d elapses.
339	func waitRemoteLink(t *testing.T, r *Runner, host Host, dev string, d time.Duration) bool {
340		t.Helper()
341		deadline := time.Now().Add(d)
342		for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
343			ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), smokeSSHTimeout)
344			_, err := r.Run(ctx, host, "ip link show "+dev)
345			cancel()
346			if err == nil {
347				return true
348			}
349			time.Sleep(300 * time.Millisecond)
350		}
351		return false
352	}
353	
354	// addressLink assigns inner/smokeInnerCIDR to the tunnel interface on host and
355	// brings it up (via sudo over SSH).
356	func addressLink(t *testing.T, r *Runner, host Host, inner string) {
357		t.Helper()
358		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), smokeSSHTimeout)
359		defer cancel()
360		// `addr replace` is idempotent: it adds the address or updates it in place,
361		// so a re-run against a still-addressed interface does not error.
362		cmd := fmt.Sprintf("sudo ip addr replace %s/%s dev %s && sudo ip link set %s up",
363			inner, smokeInnerCIDR, tunnelIface, tunnelIface)
364		if _, err := r.Run(ctx, host, cmd); err != nil {
365			t.Fatalf("%s: address %s failed: %v", host.Role, tunnelIface, err)
366		}
367		t.Logf("%s: %s addressed %s/%s up", host.Role, tunnelIface, inner, smokeInnerCIDR)
368	}
369	
370	// pingUntil pings ip from host until it replies or d elapses; the first reply
371	// implies the WireGuard session is established.
372	func pingUntil(t *testing.T, r *Runner, host Host, ip string, d time.Duration) bool {
373		t.Helper()
374		deadline := time.Now().Add(d)
375		for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
376			ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), smokeSSHTimeout)
377			_, err := r.Run(ctx, host, "ping -c 1 -W 2 "+ip)
378			cancel()
379			if err == nil {
380				return true
381			}
382			time.Sleep(500 * time.Millisecond)
383		}
384		return false
385	}
386	
387	// rttRe extracts the average from ping's "rtt min/avg/max/mdev = a/b/c/d ms"
388	// summary line.
389	var rttRe = regexp.MustCompile(`= [0-9.]+/([0-9.]+)/`)
390	
391	// measureRTT runs a 10-packet ping from host to ip and returns the average RTT
392	// in milliseconds.
393	func measureRTT(t *testing.T, r *Runner, host Host, ip string) float64 {
394		t.Helper()
395		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), smokeSSHTimeout)
396		defer cancel()
397		res, err := r.Run(ctx, host, "ping -c 10 -W 2 "+ip)
398		if err != nil {
399			t.Fatalf("%s: measured ping to %s failed: %v", host.Role, ip, err)
400		}
401		m := rttRe.FindStringSubmatch(res.Stdout)
402		if m == nil {
403			t.Fatalf("%s: could not parse RTT from ping output:\n%s", host.Role, res.Stdout)
404		}
405		avg, err := strconv.ParseFloat(m[1], 64)
406		if err != nil {
407			t.Fatalf("%s: parse avg RTT %q: %v", host.Role, m[1], err)
408		}
409		return avg
410	}
411	
412	// startIperfServer starts an iperf3 server on host bound to serverIP under a
413	// transient systemd unit, registering teardown (runs on failure too).
414	func startIperfServer(t *testing.T, r *Runner, host Host, serverIP string) {
415		t.Helper()
416		t.Cleanup(func() { stopUnit(t, r, host, iperfUnit) })
417	
418		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), smokeSSHTimeout)
419		defer cancel()
420		start := fmt.Sprintf("sudo systemd-run --unit=%s --service-type=simple iperf3 -s -B %s",
421			iperfUnit, serverIP)
422		if _, err := r.Run(ctx, host, start); err != nil {
423			t.Fatalf("%s: start iperf3 server failed: %v", host.Role, err)
424		}
425		// Give the server a moment to bind before the first client connects.
426		time.Sleep(1 * time.Second)
427		t.Logf("%s: iperf3 server up on %s (unit=%s)", host.Role, serverIP, iperfUnit)
428	}
429	
430	// tcpMeasurement is one TCP iperf3 result.
431	type tcpMeasurement struct {
432		mbps        float64
433		retransmits int
434	}
435	
436	// udpMeasurement is one UDP iperf3 result. mbps is the goodput derived from the
437	// sender rate and the measured loss (see iperfUDP), not the raw offered rate.
438	type udpMeasurement struct {
439		mbps     float64
440		lossPct  float64
441		jitterMs float64
442	}
443	
444	// iperfReport is the subset of iperf3 -J output this tier records. sum_sent
445	// carries the sender totals for TCP; sum carries the UDP summary, whose
446	// bits_per_second is the sender's offered rate (goodput is derived from
447	// lost_percent in iperfUDP), alongside jitter and loss.
448	type iperfReport struct {
449		End struct {
450			SumSent struct {
451				BitsPerSecond float64 `json:"bits_per_second"`
452				Retransmits   int     `json:"retransmits"`
453			} `json:"sum_sent"`
454			Sum struct {
455				BitsPerSecond float64 `json:"bits_per_second"`
456				JitterMs      float64 `json:"jitter_ms"`
457				LostPercent   float64 `json:"lost_percent"`
458			} `json:"sum"`
459		} `json:"end"`
460	}
461	
462	// runIperfJSON runs an iperf3 client on host and parses its -J report.
463	func runIperfJSON(t *testing.T, r *Runner, host Host, args string) iperfReport {
464		t.Helper()
465		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), iperfRunTimeout)
466		defer cancel()
467		res, err := r.Run(ctx, host, "iperf3 "+args+" -J")
468		if err != nil {
469			t.Fatalf("%s: iperf3 %s failed: %v", host.Role, args, err)
470		}
471		var rep iperfReport
472		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(res.Stdout), &rep); err != nil {
473			t.Fatalf("%s: parse iperf3 JSON: %v\n%s", host.Role, err, res.Stdout)
474		}
475		return rep
476	}
477	
478	// iperfTCP runs a 10s TCP transfer from host to serverIP with parallel streams
479	// and returns the aggregate sender goodput and retransmit count.
480	func iperfTCP(t *testing.T, r *Runner, host Host, serverIP string, parallel int) tcpMeasurement {
481		t.Helper()
482		args := fmt.Sprintf("-c %s -t 10 -P %d", serverIP, parallel)
483		rep := runIperfJSON(t, r, host, args)
484		return tcpMeasurement{
485			mbps:        rep.End.SumSent.BitsPerSecond / 1e6,
486			retransmits: rep.End.SumSent.Retransmits,
487		}
488	}
489	
490	// iperfUDP runs a 10s UDP transfer from host to serverIP at the given target
491	// bitrate and returns the goodput, loss percentage, and jitter. In iperf3's UDP
492	// client JSON, end.sum.bits_per_second is the SENDER's offered rate (it tracks
493	// the -b target), not receiver goodput, so true goodput is derived by applying
494	// the measured loss: send_rate * (1 - lost_percent/100).
495	func iperfUDP(t *testing.T, r *Runner, host Host, serverIP, bitrate string) udpMeasurement {
496		t.Helper()
497		args := fmt.Sprintf("-c %s -t 10 -u -b %s", serverIP, bitrate)
498		rep := runIperfJSON(t, r, host, args)
499		sendMbps := rep.End.Sum.BitsPerSecond / 1e6
500		lossPct := rep.End.Sum.LostPercent
501		return udpMeasurement{
502			mbps:     sendMbps * (1 - lossPct/100),
503			lossPct:  lossPct,
504			jitterMs: rep.End.Sum.JitterMs,
505		}
506	}
507	
508	// preClean removes any leftover wanbond/iperf3 units and tunnel interface from
509	// a prior interrupted run so this run starts from a clean slate. Best-effort:
510	// it logs but does not fail the test.
511	func preClean(t *testing.T, r *Runner, host Host) {
512		t.Helper()
513		stopUnit(t, r, host, smokeUnit)
514		stopUnit(t, r, host, iperfUnit)
515		delLink(t, r, host, tunnelIface)
516	}
517	
518	// stopUnit stops (and clears any failed state of) a transient systemd unit on
519	// host. Best-effort: it logs but does not fail the test, so one host's cleanup
520	// error does not mask the real result.
521	func stopUnit(t *testing.T, r *Runner, host Host, unit string) {
522		t.Helper()
523		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), cleanupTimeout)
524		defer cancel()
525		cmd := fmt.Sprintf("sudo systemctl stop %s 2>/dev/null; sudo systemctl reset-failed %s 2>/dev/null; true", unit, unit)
526		if _, err := r.Run(ctx, host, cmd); err != nil {
527			t.Logf("cleanup: %s: stop unit %s: %v", host.Role, unit, err)
528		}
529	}
530	
531	// delLink removes the tunnel interface on host. Best-effort (see stopUnit).
532	func delLink(t *testing.T, r *Runner, host Host, dev string) {
533		t.Helper()
534		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), cleanupTimeout)
535		defer cancel()
536		if _, err := r.Run(ctx, host, "sudo ip link del "+dev+" 2>/dev/null; true"); err != nil {
537			t.Logf("cleanup: %s: del link %s: %v", host.Role, dev, err)
538		}
539	}
540	
541	// removeRemoteDir deletes smokeRemoteDir on host, discarding the synced repo,
542	// the native binary, and the secret configs (WireGuard private keys + PSK) so
543	// no key material persists past the run. Best-effort (see stopUnit).
544	func removeRemoteDir(t *testing.T, r *Runner, host Host) {
545		t.Helper()
546		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), cleanupTimeout)
547		defer cancel()
548		if _, err := r.Run(ctx, host, "rm -rf "+smokeRemoteDir); err != nil {
549			t.Logf("cleanup: %s: remove %s: %v", host.Role, smokeRemoteDir, err)
550		}
551	}
552	
553	// dumpDaemonLog logs the tail of the wanbond unit's journal on host for
554	// diagnosing a failed bring-up.
555	func dumpDaemonLog(t *testing.T, r *Runner, host Host) {
556		t.Helper()
557		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), smokeSSHTimeout)
558		defer cancel()
559		res, err := r.Run(ctx, host, "sudo journalctl -u "+smokeUnit+" --no-pager -n 50 2>&1; true")
560		if err != nil {
561			t.Logf("%s: could not read daemon journal: %v", host.Role, err)
562			return
563		}
564		t.Logf("%s: wanbond journal (last 50 lines):\n%s", host.Role, res.Stdout)
565	}
566	
567	// genSmokeKey generates an X25519 keypair (WireGuard key format), base64-encoded
568	// as the TOML config carries it.
569	func genSmokeKey(t *testing.T) (privB64, pubB64 string) {
570		t.Helper()
571		priv, err := ecdh.X25519().GenerateKey(rand.Reader)
572		if err != nil {
573			t.Fatalf("generate X25519 key: %v", err)
574		}
575		return base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(priv.Bytes()),
576			base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(priv.PublicKey().Bytes())
577	}
578	
579	// randSmokeKey returns 32 random bytes base64-encoded (the outer-control PSK).
580	func randSmokeKey(t *testing.T) string {
581		t.Helper()
582		var b [32]byte
583		if _, err := io.ReadFull(rand.Reader, b[:]); err != nil {
584			t.Fatalf("read random: %v", err)
585		}
586		return base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(b[:])
587	}

1	package config
2	
3	import (
4		"errors"
5		"fmt"
6		"net"
7		"net/netip"
8		"net/url"
9		"strings"
10		"time"
11	
12		"github.com/7mind/wanbond/internal/dnsresolve"
13	)
14	
15	// DNSResolverMode selects the transport dns.resolver picks.
16	type DNSResolverMode string
17	
18	const (
19		// DNSResolverSystem uses the OS stub resolver (dnsresolve.SystemResolver).
20		// It is the default when [dns] is omitted or resolver is left empty.
21		DNSResolverSystem DNSResolverMode = "system"
22		// DNSResolverDoH uses DNS-over-HTTPS (dnsresolve.DoHResolver).
23		DNSResolverDoH DNSResolverMode = "doh"
24		// DNSResolverDoT uses DNS-over-TLS (dnsresolve.DoTResolver).
25		DNSResolverDoT DNSResolverMode = "dot"
26	)
27	
28	func (m DNSResolverMode) valid() bool {
29		return m == DNSResolverSystem || m == DNSResolverDoH || m == DNSResolverDoT
30	}
31	
32	// Default cadence/timeout for the [dns] block (Q31): the poll interval sits on
33	// the reconcile-cadence scale — frequent enough that a DDNS repoint is picked
34	// up promptly, far coarser than the sub-second deferred-path/probe loops
35	// (bind.DefaultReconcileInterval = 1s) since re-resolution costs a real
36	// network round trip to the configured resolver and re-resolving too eagerly
37	// would needlessly load it. The per-lookup timeout mirrors dnsresolve's own
38	// internal DoH/DoT bounds (5s).
39	const (
40		defaultDNSPollInterval = 30 * time.Second
41		defaultDNSTimeout      = 5 * time.Second
42	)
43	
44	// dotDefaultPort mirrors dnsresolve's unexported dotPort: DNS-over-TLS's
45	// IANA-assigned port (RFC 7858 §3), and the ONLY port dnsresolve.NewDoTResolver
46	// ever dials (it appends this fixed port itself). An explicit port in
47	// dot_server must match it, so a mistyped non-standard port fails fast at
48	// config load rather than being silently ignored when the resolver is built.
49	const dotDefaultPort = "853"
50	
51	// DNS selects and tunes the resolver transport used for opted-in hostname
52	// peer-endpoint re-resolution (Q29/Q31). An ABSENT [dns] block is INERT: it
53	// defaults to the system resolver, and resolution only happens at all when a
54	// peer separately opts in with its own `dns = true` flag (Peer.DNS) —
55	// [dns] merely selects which transport that opt-in uses, it is never a gate
56	// by itself.
57	type DNS struct {
58		// Resolver selects the transport: "system" (default), "doh", or "dot".
59		Resolver DNSResolverMode `toml:"resolver"`
60		// DoHURL is the DNS-over-HTTPS query endpoint (an https:// URL). Required
61		// iff resolver = "doh"; rejected (must stay empty) under any other
62		// resolver.
63		DoHURL string `toml:"doh_url"`
64		// DoTServer is the DNS-over-TLS server: a bare host, or "host:port" where
65		// port must be dotDefaultPort (853) — dnsresolve.NewDoTResolver always
66		// dials that port itself, so any other explicit port cannot be honored.
67		// Required iff resolver = "dot"; rejected (must stay empty) under any
68		// other resolver.
69		DoTServer string `toml:"dot_server"`
70		// BootstrapIP is the literal IP address wanbond dials the DoH/DoT server
71		// on when doh_url/dot_server names it by HOSTNAME rather than IP literal
72		// (the chicken-and-egg BOOTSTRAP-IP invariant, Q33): resolving the
73		// private resolver's own name would itself require a DNS lookup — via
74		// the system resolver [dns] exists to avoid — defeating the point of
75		// configuring a private resolver. Required iff the doh_url/dot_server
76		// host is a hostname; rejected (must stay empty) when the host is
77		// already an IP literal — there is nothing to bootstrap in that case, so
78		// a non-empty value there is a mode mismatch, rejected fail-fast like the
79		// other stray-field checks in validate.
80		BootstrapIP string `toml:"bootstrap_ip"`
81		// PollInterval is the re-resolution cadence for an opted-in hostname peer
82		// endpoint (Q31), parsed from PollIntervalRaw in applyDefaults. Defaults to
83		// defaultDNSPollInterval when PollIntervalRaw is left empty; must be > 0
84		// after defaulting.
85		PollInterval time.Duration `toml:"-"`
86		// PollIntervalRaw is the TOML Go-duration string form of PollInterval,
87		// e.g. "30s". Mirrors Path.LinkRTTRaw: go-toml/v2 cannot decode a TOML
88		// string directly into a time.Duration field, so the raw string is parsed
89		// via time.ParseDuration in applyDefaults.
90		PollIntervalRaw string `toml:"poll_interval"`
91		// Timeout bounds a single resolver lookup, parsed from TimeoutRaw in
92		// applyDefaults. Defaults to defaultDNSTimeout when TimeoutRaw is left
93		// empty; must be > 0 after defaulting.
94		Timeout time.Duration `toml:"-"`
95		// TimeoutRaw is the TOML Go-duration string form of Timeout, e.g. "5s".
96		TimeoutRaw string `toml:"timeout"`
97	}
98	
99	// applyDefaults fills the resolver mode and cadence/timeout knobs left at
100	// their zero value, so a minimal or absent [dns] block resolves to explicit,
101	// usable settings — an absent block still ends up as the system resolver
102	// with the standard cadence/timeout, per the type's zero-value contract.
103	// PollIntervalRaw/TimeoutRaw are parsed here (mirroring Path's *Raw-field
104	// normalize step for LinkRTTRaw); an unparseable duration string is reported
105	// immediately.
106	func (d *DNS) applyDefaults() error {
107		if d.Resolver == "" {
108			d.Resolver = DNSResolverSystem
109		}
110		if d.PollIntervalRaw == "" {
111			d.PollInterval = defaultDNSPollInterval
112		} else {
113			v, err := time.ParseDuration(d.PollIntervalRaw)
114			if err != nil {
115				return fmt.Errorf("dns.poll_interval: invalid duration %q: %w", d.PollIntervalRaw, err)
116			}
117			d.PollInterval = v
118		}
119		if d.TimeoutRaw == "" {
120			d.Timeout = defaultDNSTimeout
121		} else {
122			v, err := time.ParseDuration(d.TimeoutRaw)
123			if err != nil {
124				return fmt.Errorf("dns.timeout: invalid duration %q: %w", d.TimeoutRaw, err)
125			}
126			d.Timeout = v
127		}
128		return nil
129	}
130	
131	// validate enforces the [dns] block invariants: a resolver-mode-appropriate
132	// set of required fields, no stray fields left over from a different mode,
133	// positive cadence/timeout, and the BOOTSTRAP-IP invariant for a hostname-form
134	// doh_url/dot_server. Runs after applyDefaults (mirroring Amnezia/FEC/
135	// SchedulerConfig), so Resolver/PollInterval/Timeout are already defaulted.
136	func (d DNS) validate() error {
137		if !d.Resolver.valid() {
138			return fmt.Errorf("dns.resolver must be %q, %q or %q, got %q", DNSResolverSystem, DNSResolverDoH, DNSResolverDoT, d.Resolver)
139		}
140		if d.PollInterval <= 0 {
141			return fmt.Errorf("dns.poll_interval must be > 0, got %s", d.PollInterval)
142		}
143		if d.Timeout <= 0 {
144			return fmt.Errorf("dns.timeout must be > 0, got %s", d.Timeout)
145		}
146	
147		switch d.Resolver {
148		case DNSResolverSystem:
149			if d.DoHURL != "" {
150				return errors.New("dns.doh_url is only meaningful when dns.resolver = \"doh\"")
151			}
152			if d.DoTServer != "" {
153				return errors.New("dns.dot_server is only meaningful when dns.resolver = \"dot\"")
154			}
155			if d.BootstrapIP != "" {
156				return errors.New("dns.bootstrap_ip is only meaningful when dns.resolver is \"doh\" or \"dot\"")
157			}
158			return nil
159		case DNSResolverDoH:
160			if d.DoTServer != "" {
161				return errors.New("dns.dot_server is not meaningful when dns.resolver = \"doh\"")
162			}
163			if d.DoHURL == "" {
164				return errors.New("dns.doh_url is required when dns.resolver = \"doh\"")
165			}
166			host, err := dohURLHost(d.DoHURL)
167			if err != nil {
168				return fmt.Errorf("dns.doh_url: %w", err)
169			}
170			return d.requireBootstrapForHost(host)
171		case DNSResolverDoT:
172			if d.DoHURL != "" {
173				return errors.New("dns.doh_url is not meaningful when dns.resolver = \"dot\"")
174			}
175			if d.DoTServer == "" {
176				return errors.New("dns.dot_server is required when dns.resolver = \"dot\"")
177			}
178			host, err := dotServerHost(d.DoTServer)
179			if err != nil {
180				return fmt.Errorf("dns.dot_server: %w", err)
181			}
182			return d.requireBootstrapForHost(host)
183		default:
184			// Unreachable: d.Resolver.valid() above already rejected anything else.
185			return fmt.Errorf("dns.resolver: unhandled mode %q", d.Resolver)
186		}
187	}
188	
189	// requireBootstrapForHost enforces the BOOTSTRAP-IP invariant for the given
190	// (already-extracted) doh_url/dot_server host: an IP-literal host needs no
191	// bootstrap and rejects a stray bootstrap_ip as a mode mismatch (mirroring
192	// the doh_url/dot_server stray-field checks in validate — NewResolver would
193	// otherwise silently pin the dial to bootstrap_ip instead of the configured
194	// host); a hostname requires an explicit, valid bootstrap_ip.
195	func (d DNS) requireBootstrapForHost(host string) error {
196		if _, err := netip.ParseAddr(host); err == nil {
197			if d.BootstrapIP != "" {
198				return fmt.Errorf("dns.bootstrap_ip is not meaningful when the doh_url/dot_server host %q is already an IP literal", host)
199			}
200			return nil
201		}
202		if d.BootstrapIP == "" {
203			return fmt.Errorf("hostname %q requires dns.bootstrap_ip: the resolver's own address must be reachable without a DNS lookup (a plaintext lookup of the private resolver's own name would defeat the point of configuring one)", host)
204		}
205		if _, err := netip.ParseAddr(d.BootstrapIP); err != nil {
206			return fmt.Errorf("dns.bootstrap_ip %q is not a valid IP literal: %w", d.BootstrapIP, err)
207		}
208		return nil
209	}
210	
211	// dohURLHost extracts and validates the host component of a doh_url: it must
212	// be a well-formed https:// URL with a non-empty host.
213	func dohURLHost(rawURL string) (string, error) {
214		u, err := url.Parse(rawURL)
215		if err != nil {
216			return "", fmt.Errorf("invalid URL %q: %w", rawURL, err)
217		}
218		if u.Scheme != "https" {
219			return "", fmt.Errorf("must use https, got scheme %q in %q", u.Scheme, rawURL)
220		}
221		host := u.Hostname()
222		if host == "" {
223			return "", fmt.Errorf("missing host in %q", rawURL)
224		}
225		return host, nil
226	}
227	
228	// dotServerHost extracts and validates the host component of a dot_server: a
229	// bare host (hostname or IP literal, no port) is accepted as-is; a
230	// "host:port" form is split and requires port == dotDefaultPort, since
231	// dnsresolve.NewDoTResolver always dials that fixed port itself.
232	func dotServerHost(raw string) (string, error) {
233		if raw == "" {
234			return "", errors.New("empty")
235		}
236		// A bare IP literal (including an unbracketed IPv6 literal, which itself
237		// contains colons) has no port to split off.
238		if _, err := netip.ParseAddr(raw); err == nil {
239			return raw, nil
240		}
241		if !strings.Contains(raw, ":") {
242			return raw, nil // bare hostname, no port
243		}
244		host, port, err := net.SplitHostPort(raw)
245		if err != nil {
246			return "", fmt.Errorf("invalid dot_server %q: %w", raw, err)
247		}
248		if port != dotDefaultPort {
249			return "", fmt.Errorf("port %q must be %q (the IANA-assigned DNS-over-TLS port; dnsresolve always dials it)", port, dotDefaultPort)
250		}
251		return host, nil
252	}
253	
254	// NewResolver constructs the dnsresolve.Resolver implementation the
255	// (validated) [dns] block selects: dnsresolve.NewSystemResolver for the
256	// default/system mode, dnsresolve.NewDoHResolver/NewDoTResolver for doh/dot,
257	// or their *WithBootstrap variants when bootstrap_ip is set. Callers
258	// normally obtain a DNS value via config.Load, which already ran
259	// applyDefaults/validate; calling this on an un-validated DNS value surfaces
260	// whatever the underlying dnsresolve constructor rejects.
261	//
262	// bootstrap_ip (validated fail-fast, see requireBootstrapForHost) IS wired
263	// into the constructed resolver's dial target: when set, the resolver dials
264	// bootstrap_ip directly instead of resolving the configured hostname through
265	// the system dialer — the BOOTSTRAP-IP invariant (Q33) exists precisely to
266	// avoid that plaintext lookup of the private resolver's own name.
267	func (d DNS) NewResolver() (dnsresolve.Resolver, error) {
268		switch d.Resolver {
269		case DNSResolverDoH:
270			if d.BootstrapIP != "" {
271				return dnsresolve.NewDoHResolverWithBootstrap(d.DoHURL, d.BootstrapIP)
272			}
273			return dnsresolve.NewDoHResolver(d.DoHURL)
274		case DNSResolverDoT:
275			host, err := dotServerHost(d.DoTServer)
276			if err != nil {
277				return nil, fmt.Errorf("dns.dot_server: %w", err)
278			}
279			if d.BootstrapIP != "" {
280				return dnsresolve.NewDoTResolverWithBootstrap(host, d.BootstrapIP)
281			}
282			return dnsresolve.NewDoTResolver(host)
283		default:
284			return dnsresolve.NewSystemResolver(), nil
285		}
286	}

1	//go:build realhosts
2	
3	// Package realhosts holds the real-host end-to-end tier: it drives the two
4	// standing worker machines (the amd64 edge behind a symmetric NAT and the
5	// aarch64 concentrator with a public inbound IP) over SSH. It is guarded by the
6	// dedicated `realhosts` build tag — FULLY SEPARATE from the netns `e2e` tag — so
7	// neither the default unprivileged `go build ./...` / `go test ./...` nor the
8	// privileged `-tags e2e` netns suite compiles or runs any of it.
9	//
10	// The tier is REPORT-ONLY: it executes commands on the real hosts and records
11	// their output (connectivity, uname/arch, and — in later tasks — a synced,
12	// freshly built wanbond binary). It gates nothing; the netns `e2e` fixture
13	// remains the authoritative pass/fail harness. It COMPLEMENTS that fixture by
14	// exercising the actual internet path between the real NAT and the real public
15	// endpoint.
16	//
17	// Invoke it only via the opt-in `just realhosts` target; it is never part of
18	// `just test` or CI.
19	package realhosts
20
